text: The dim lighting and empty halls create an atmosphere of stillness and seclusion. I feel a heightened awareness of the structures around me, the way artificial light struggles against darkness. There is something contemplative about the silence, and yet a small unease—spaces meant for people now vacant, like waiting rooms for forgotten thoughts. I feel both curious and ever-so-slightly unsettled, as if I am bearing witness to the echo of activity that once filled this place.
